old_claim.note: Radiology Departments provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Clinical Medicine provides the patient-diagnosis and medical-interpretation context for applying electrocardiograms to heart disease.

source_shape.source_locator: National MagLab says Einthoven published patient ECG examples for heart abnormalities, studied healthy-heart patterns to detect disease differences, and enhanced diagnosis of heart problems.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Einthoven's electrocardiography was a clinical diagnostic practice for interpreting heart disease, not a radiology-department workflow.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source ties ECG to patient heart abnormalities, healthy-heart comparison, and diagnosis; radiology departments are an imaging specialty and are not the clinical substrate described for early ECG.

ontology_before: Early Electrocardiography depended on radiology_departments, incorrectly routing an electrical physiological recording method through X-ray imaging infrastructure.
ontology_after: Early Electrocardiography depends on medicine_clinical for patient diagnosis and disease interpretation, while laboratory_diagnostic_medicine covers the fixed instrument/lab context.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: electrocardiography_early now depends on medicine_clinical and no longer directly depends on radiology_departments.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped to ECG deployment inside radiology departments rather than early electrocardiography.
A stronger source shows early Einthoven ECG practice required X-ray/radiology infrastructure.
A dedicated clinical_electrophysiology node is added and becomes the narrower prerequisite.
short_reason: ECG was clinical physiological diagnosis, not radiology.
